title: lonicera involucrata var. ledebourii
intro: Lonicera involucrata var. ledebourii, also known as the twinflower, is a perennial plant species belonging to the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The twinflower has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Herbal preparations of Lonicera involucrata var. ledebourii, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, have been used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
